Hospital.title: Hospital
Hospital.description: Represents a healthcare facility within the system, serving as the multi-tenancy anchor for all other data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Hospital entity. This ID is used as 'hospitalId' in other entities for multi-tenancy.

name.description: The official name of the hospital.

address.description: The physical address of the hospital.

contactNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the hospital.

email.description: The general email address for the hospital.

website.description: The official website URL for the hospital.

licenceNumber.description: The official operating license number issued to the hospital, relevant for Ghanaian regulations.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the hospital record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the hospital record was last updated.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user account in the system, linked to Firebase Authentication, and defines their role and basic profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically corresponding to Firebase Authentication's 'uid'.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and communication.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number, used for communication, multi-factor authentication, and SMS reminders.

role.description: The role of the user, determining their access permissions within the system.

isActive.description: A flag indicating whether the user account is currently active.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Stores comprehensive demographic and administrative information for a patient, optimized for search.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Patient entity (Unique Patient ID).

userProfileId.description: Optional reference to UserProfile if the patient also has a system login. (Relationship: UserProfile 0:1 Patient)

contactNumber.description: The patient's primary contact phone number.

emergencyContactName.description: The name of the patient's emergency contact person.

emergencyContactNumber.description: The phone number of the patient's emergency contact person.

ghanaCardId.description: The patient's Ghana Card ID number, for local identification and compliance.

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the patient was first registered in the system.

allergies.description: A list of known allergies the patient has.

currentMedications.description: A list of medications the patient is currently taking.

medicalHistory.description: A summary or general overview of the patient's medical history.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the patient record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the patient record was last updated.

Department.title: Department
Department.description: Defines a medical or administrative department within a hospital.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Department entity.

headOfDepartmentId.description: Optional reference to the UserProfile of the staff member (e.g., Doctor, Admin) who heads this department. (Relationship: UserProfile 0:1 Department)

description.description: A brief description of the department's function.

contactNumber.description: The direct contact phone number for the department.

location.description: The physical location of the department within the hospital (e.g., 'Ground Floor, West Wing').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the department record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the department record was last updated.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Records details of a scheduled patient appointment with a staff member or department.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient attending this appointment. (Relationship: Patient 1:N Appointment)

assignedStaffId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the staff member (e.g., Doctor, Nurse) assigned to this appointment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Appointment)

departmentId.description: Optional reference to the Department where the appointment is taking place. (Relationship: Department 1:N Appointment)

appointmentDateTime.description: The exact date and time when the appointment is scheduled to begin.

durationMinutes.description: The planned duration of the appointment in minutes.

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., 'Scheduled', 'Confirmed', 'Completed', 'Canceled', 'No Show').

reasonForVisit.description: The patient's stated reason or complaint for the visit.

notes.description: Any internal notes or specific instructions related to the appointment.

reminderSent.description: A flag indicating whether an automated reminder has been sent for this appointment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the appointment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the appointment record was last updated.

StaffSchedule.title: StaffSchedule
StaffSchedule.description: Defines the recurring availability or work schedule for a specific staff member.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StaffSchedule entity.

staffId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the staff member whose schedule this is. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N StaffSchedule)

dayOfWeek.description: The day of the week for which this schedule applies (e.g., 'Monday', 'Tuesday').

startTime.description: The start time of the availability slot on the specified day (e.g., '09:00:00').

endTime.description: The end time of the availability slot on the specified day (e.g., '17:00:00').

isAvailable.description: A flag indicating if the staff member is generally available during this slot.

departmentId.description: Optional reference to the Department where the staff member is scheduled to work during this time. (Relationship: Department 1:N StaffSchedule)

validFrom.description: The date from which this schedule entry becomes valid. Useful for managing changing schedules.

validTo.description: The date until which this schedule entry is valid. Useful for managing changing schedules.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the staff schedule record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the staff schedule record was last updated.

HospitalResource.title: HospitalResource
HospitalResource.description: Represents a bookable resource within a hospital, such as an operating theater or diagnostic equipment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HospitalResource entity.

name.description: The name of the resource (e.g., 'Operating Theater 1', 'MRI Scanner').

type.description: The category or type of the resource (e.g., 'Operating Theater', 'Diagnostic Equipment', 'Consultation Room', 'Lab Equipment', 'Ward Bed').

description.description: A detailed description of the resource, including capabilities or specifications.

location.description: The physical location of the resource within the hospital.

isBookable.description: A flag indicating whether this resource can be scheduled or booked.

capacity.description: The capacity of the resource, if applicable (e.g., number of beds in a ward, max occupants for a room).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the resource record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the resource record was last updated.

ResourceBooking.title: ResourceBooking
ResourceBooking.description: Records a specific booking for a hospital resource over a period of time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ResourceBooking entity.

resourceId.description: Reference to the HospitalResource being booked. (Relationship: HospitalResource 1:N ResourceBooking)

bookingStartDateTime.description: The date and time when the resource booking officially starts.

bookingEndDateTime.description: The date and time when the resource booking officially ends.

bookedById.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the staff member who made this booking. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ResourceBooking)

patientId.description: Optional reference to the Patient for whom this resource is being booked (e.g., for a surgery). (Relationship: Patient 1:N ResourceBooking)

purpose.description: The purpose or reason for booking the resource (e.g., 'Emergency Surgery', 'MRI Scan', 'Routine Consultation').

status.description: The current status of the resource booking (e.g., 'Confirmed', 'Pending', 'Canceled', 'Completed').

notes.description: Any additional notes or specific requirements for the resource booking.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the resource booking record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the resource booking record was last updated.

definition.description: Top-level collection for each hospital instance. The '{hospitalId}' parameter acts as the multi-tenancy key for all nested data. Includes denormalized 'id' as 'hospitalId'.

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: Unique identifier for the hospital, serving as the multi-tenancy anchor.

definition.description: Subcollection containing user profiles for staff and patients within a specific hospital. 'userId' corresponds to Firebase Auth UID. Includes denormalized 'hospitalId' (from path) and 'role' for RBAC.

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: Unique identifier of the hospital this user profile belongs to.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier for the user profile, matching the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Subcollection storing comprehensive patient demographic and administrative information for a specific hospital. Includes denormalized 'hospitalId' (from path).

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: Unique identifier of the hospital the patient is registered with.

params.name: patientId
params.description: Unique identifier for the patient record.

definition.description: Subcollection defining medical or administrative departments within a specific hospital. Includes denormalized 'hospitalId' (from path).

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: Unique identifier of the hospital the department belongs to.

definition.description: Subcollection recording scheduled patient appointments within a specific hospital. Includes denormalized 'hospitalId' (from path), 'patientId', and 'assignedStaffId' for access control.

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: Unique identifier of the hospital where the appointment is scheduled.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: Unique identifier for the appointment record.

definition.description: Subcollection defining recurring availability or work schedules for staff members within a specific hospital. Includes denormalized 'hospitalId' (from path) and 'staffId' for ownership.

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: Unique identifier of the hospital the staff schedule belongs to.

params.name: scheduleId
params.description: Unique identifier for the staff schedule entry.

definition.description: Subcollection representing bookable resources (e.g., operating theaters, equipment) within a specific hospital. Includes denormalized 'hospitalId' (from path).

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: Unique identifier of the hospital the resource belongs to.

params.name: resourceId
params.description: Unique identifier for the hospital resource.

definition.description: Subcollection recording bookings for hospital resources within a specific hospital. Includes denormalized 'hospitalId' (from path) and 'bookedById' for ownership.

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: Unique identifier of the hospital where the resource is booked.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: Unique identifier for the resource booking record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure implements a robust multi-tenancy model by nesting all tenant-specific data under a top-level `/hospitals/{hospitalId}/` collection. This design strategy directly addresses the 'Authorization Independence' and 'QAPs (Rules are not Filters)' mandates, while adhering to the principle of 'DBAC (No Custom Claims)'.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

1.  **Path-Based Multi-Tenancy:** By structuring all collections as subcollections of `/hospitals/{hospitalId}/`, the `hospitalId` (the multi-tenancy anchor) becomes an explicit path parameter (`{hospitalId}`). This means for any operation on documents within a specific hospital, the `hospitalId` is directly available in the security rules context (`hospitalId` wildcard). This eliminates the need to perform a `get()` operation on a parent `Hospital` document to determine its attributes, thus ensuring authorization independence at the entity level.

2.  **Denormalized `hospitalId` Field:** Although the `hospitalId` is present in the path, every document within a hospital's scope (e.g., `userProfiles`, `patients`, `appointments`) will also explicitly include a `hospitalId` field. This denormalization enhances data integrity, allows for more flexible querying (e.g., if a flat collection structure were ever needed for specific use cases), and explicitly fulfills the user's requirement to include `hospitalId` in every document.

3.  **User Profile for Role-Based Access:** Each user's profile is stored at `/hospitals/{hospitalId}/userProfiles/{userId}`, where `userId` is the Firebase Authentication `uid`. This `UserProfile` document contains the user's `role` and confirms their association with a specific `hospitalId`. To authorize access to any other document, security rules will perform a single `get()` operation on the *requesting user's own `UserProfile`* (e.g., `get(/hospitals/$(hospitalId)/userProfiles/$(request.auth.uid))`). This lookup retrieves the user's `hospitalId` (implicitly from the path) and `role` *for that specific hospital*. This `get()` for the current user's identity/role is a standard and acceptable practice in Firestore security rules for RBAC, and it does not violate the spirit of 'Authorization Independence' which primarily targets avoiding `get()` on *other entity documents' attributes* to secure subcollections.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

1.  **Implicit Filtering by Path:** The `/hospitals/{hospitalId}/` structure intrinsically enforces Query Against Path segments (QAPs) for multi-tenancy. When a client queries `/hospitals/hosp_abc_123/patients`, the security rules can easily ensure that `request.path.segments[1]` (which is `hosp_abc_123`) matches the `hospitalId` of the current authenticated user's profile. This means users cannot accidentally or maliciously list documents from other hospitals because their query must specify the `hospitalId` they belong to, and the rules will validate this.

2.  **Role-Based Filtering for Operations:** Once `hospitalId` is validated, the `UserProfile.role` (retrieved via the single `get()` on the user's own profile) enables granular access control for `list`, `get`, `create`, `update`, and `delete` operations. For example, a Doctor might be able to `list` and `get` `Patient` records, but only an Admin might be able to `create`, `update`, or `delete` them, all strictly within their validated `hospitalId`. Patients can only read their own appointments by checking `resource.data.patientId` against their linked patient ID in their `UserProfile`. This ensures that `list` queries are not used as a backdoor to bypass security rules, as the rules enforce necessary filters based on both `hospitalId` and user role *before* any data is returned.

This architecture provides a scalable, secure, and debuggable foundation for the MedFlow GH system, accommodating multi-tenancy and robust RBAC while adhering to the specified design principles.
